checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=69199000 (2016-12-31) vs AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=70597000 (2015-12-31), a decline, while Revenues=671144000 (2016) vs Revenues=664994000 (2015), an increase

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: Revenues=659214000 (2013 annual) vs Revenues=638456000 (2012 annual), a 3.25% increase, while OtherAssetsNoncurrent increased over 460% in the same period

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?
Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: No InventoryNet or CostOfGoodsSold line items were provided in the dataset (trucking company, asset-light on inventory); OperatingIncomeLoss=58303000 (2016 annual) vs Revenues=671144000 (2016 annual) implies ~8.7% operating margin

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.location: Income Statement - quarterly and annual NI

evidence.quote: Revenues=161929000 (2016 Q1) growing to 172661000 (2016 Q4), a ~6.6% sequential increase, while NetIncomeLoss stayed within a narrow 8.2-8.5M band each quarter
evidence.location: Income Statement - quarterly Revenue vs NI

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.location: Balance Sheet - Allowance for Doubtful Accounts

evidence.quote: Revenues=664994000 (2015 annual) vs Revenues=671144000 (2016 annual), a slight increase, while the allowance declined from 305000 to 275000 over the same period

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: 10-K filed 2012-03-14, 2013-03-14, 2014-03-12, 2015-03-12, 2016-03-11, 2017-03-15 — consistent annual filing cadence with no NT 10-K/NT 10-Q or 10-K/A amendments during the 2010-2017 review period

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Annual data provided for Revenues, NetIncomeLoss, OperatingIncomeLoss, NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities, Assets, Liabilities, AccountsReceivableNetCurrent, AccountsPayableCurrent, AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent spanning fiscal years 2009-2016 with supplementary quarterly detail 2010-2016
No inventory, intangible asset, deferred revenue, or segment-level data was provided, limiting depth of CL2 and CL4 analysis

top_signals: Operating cash flow consistently and substantially exceeds net income (e.g., NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=133566000 vs NetIncomeLoss=33464000 in 2016), which is expected for a capital-intensive trucking company with heavy depreciation add-backs and is a conservative rather than aggressive signal

Receivables and revenue growth track closely together over most periods, with receivables actually declining in 2015-2016 while revenue grew modestly, arguing against channel-stuffing or premature revenue recognition
No late filings, NT forms, or financial statement amendments (10-K/A, 10-Q/A) during the 2010-2017 review window; consistent ~10-11 week post-year-end 10-K filing cadence

Minor immaterial anomalies exist (a one-time jump in OtherAssetsNoncurrent in 2013 from ~$0.6M to ~$3.4M, and a mild decline in the doubtful-accounts allowance in 2015-2016) but these are too small in dollar terms relative to total assets (~$650M) and revenue (~$670M) to indicate material misstatement risk
